text:
Market today:
The mood is cautious, driven by Dallas Fed comments suggesting 'modestly' higher interest rates. This is a negative signal for your tech and chip names, as higher rates increase borrowing costs and can devalue future earnings.

Your holdings:
SOXL (-44.92%) and SPAL (-41.27%) are your most concerning positions. Both are leveraged ETFs, prone to decay, and showing significant losses. QQQ, SMH, and QQQI are all on 'TRIM' status with small losses. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch:
Keep a close eye on interest rate sentiment. Any further hawkish comments could pressure your tech-heavy book. Also, monitor your stop levels on all leveraged positions critically.

Bottom line:
Focus intensely on managing risk in your leveraged and concentrated tech positions, especially with rising rate concerns.
plays: SOXL: Consider exiting this leveraged ETF, given its 'SELL' status and deep -44.92% loss. It decays and is highly volatile.
SPAL: Review your stop at 21.38 for this new, leveraged ETF. Its -41.27% loss is substantial and margin amplifies risk.
QQQ/SMH/QQQI: Respect 'TRIM' status on these core tech holdings. Consider reducing exposure to manage concentration.
